text.name: gui_voice_pack_warn_message
text.text: You are playing Fields of Stories without voice pack (optional). Required version: %s

text.name: gui_voice_pack_warn_ok_notification
text.text: Voice pack OK: version matches Fields of Stories.

text.name: gui_mapplaces_gen_title
text.text: Fields of Stories – Generating map places

text.name: gui_mapplaces_gen_message
text.text: Experimental: No saved configuration was found for this map. If you continue, "Fields of Stories" will automatically create hundreds to thousands of places on the map. The game will appear frozen until generation finishes.

text.name: gui_mapplaces_gen_message2
text.text:
For a better experience, drive around the map and add your own places (Shift + F3) or remove incorrect ones.
For more information, open the in-game Help menu and read the Fields of Stories topics.

text.name: gui_mapplaces_gen_before_load
text.text: Press OK to start loading and generating map places. The game may appear frozen until this finishes. This is normal.

text.name: gui_firstload_tutorial_message
text.text: Follow this guide to get the most out of the mod. For full details on every feature, open the in-game Help menu and read the Fields of Stories topics.

text.name: gui_firstload_tutorial_message2
text.text: 1. Mod activation: The mod will be loaded automatically. In the first minutes, there may be performance limitations as many vehicles and data are loaded.

text.name: gui_firstload_tutorial_message3
text.text: 2. Place generation: On mod maps, the automatic place generation has already started to recognize houses, roads, and forests automatically. Follow the guide in the in-game Help menu (Setup category) to get the best experience on mod maps.

text.name: gui_firstload_tutorial_message4
text.text: 3. Let the experiences come to you: It's best not to directly pull everything out of Fields-of-Stories. Of course, you experience how you meet characters in the world, receive calls when you least expect it, and drive over the map and discover the farmyards.

text.name: gui_firstload_tutorial_message5
text.text: 4. Support the farmers: Be friendly and helpful, then you will long-term good relationships and many benefits.

text.name: gui_firstload_tutorial_message6
text.text: 5. Note the roadmap: The mod is still a small part of the vision of the project: -Helpers and contractors, -moving and driving characters, -large events, -dozens of mission types, all of this is possible, but it takes time and your support.

text.name: gui_phone_incoming_notification
text.text: Incoming call from %s. Open your phone to answer or decline.

text.name: gui_mapinit_dialog_message
text.text: Add and remove places for character situations. Note the recommended amount shown in the list. First, select the place size at the top (on foot, with vehicle, or vehicle with implement).

text.name: gui_mapinit_characters_message
text.text: Review the character details and configure them.

text.name: gui_mapinit_general_actions_message
text.text: Features to help you configure or cleanup your map.

text.name: gui_mapinit_help_info_body
text.text:
The full Fields of Stories guide lives in the in-game Help menu.
Press Esc to open the menu, go to Help, and look under the 'Roleplay' and 'Setup' categories to read about every feature - meeting neighbours, conversations, relationships, field contracts, homebases, creating map places and more.
This Settings tab holds the place and character tools for setting up a map.

text.name: gui_help_overview_body
text.text:
Fields of Stories turns your game world into a living neighbourhood. The people around you are not anonymous NPCs: they live in their own homes, follow daily routines, work their fields, and have different relationships with you.
What you can do: - Meet neighbours out in the world and talk to them face to face. - Take calls when a farmer needs help with fieldwork. - Accept field contracts, optionally borrowing the neighbour's machinery. - Improve your personal relationship with each character over time.
This Help section explains every feature in detail.

text.name: gui_help_characters_body
text.text:
These are the neighbours in your world. Each one has their own personality, role and job, as well as their own backstory.
Roles you may meet include farmers, a forester, the mayor, a machinery dealer, an agricultural contractor, a grain trader, family members and ordinary civilians. A character's role and job determine what they do all day and what you can talk about with them.
Spend time with the characters whose stories interest you the most.

text.name: gui_help_places_body
text.text:
Places are the anchors that bring a map to life. They tell the mod where characters can stand, park, shop, relax and live. On the supported maps this is already set up; this topic is for your own maps.
How to create a place: 1. Open the menu with SHIFT+F3 and go to the Settings tab. 2. Pick a place size at the top. 3. Stand (or park your vehicle) exactly where the place should be. 4. Click the place type you want. The current position is saved and you get a 'Place saved' confirmation. The more places you save, the more dynamic and unique the gameplay becomes.
Place sizes: - Character: on foot only, for standing situations without vehicles. - Vehicle: room for one car or tractor. - Vehicle with implement: a vehicle plus a trailer or implement (optional). - Oversize vehicle (homebase only): an extra-wide parking spot for combines and self-propelled harvesters. - Large area: a wide, open area for situations that need space, such as homebase machine preparation, markets or events.
Common place types and their purpose: - Character homebase: where a neighbour lives (see 'Homebases'). - Shop, gas station, workshop, shed, sell point, industry, public infrastructure: functional public spots. - Roadside, dirt road, trees: roadside encounters. - Public place and public relax place: social and leisure spots; private relax place is for quieter routines. - Player farm: events on your own farm.
Map generator for new maps: On an unknown map the generator can create many places automatically from the map data. Be patient: a large number of places may be created and the game can look frozen for a moment while it works.
Ready-to-use map configurations: for many maps you can download a finished setup, or upload and share your own via the official Website of the Mod. This can be found through the Mod´s Github page.

text.name: gui_help_char_desc_21
text.text:
Role: Neighbour | Job: Farmer Age: 31–50 | Relationship: Known | Behaviour: Stressed, Professional
Brian is a comparatively young farm owner who has taken over the family business and is attempting to reorient it using modern methods and technologies. His focus lies on efficiency, animal health, and data-driven management—strategies that lead him to rely heavily on sensor technology, digital systems, and optimized workflows.
In his day-to-day life, Brian often appears tense, as he is tasked with simultaneously maintaining the stability of the business and undertaking its comprehensive modernization. This transition entails organizational pressure, financial strain, and—quite frequently—resistance from those around him or from his own family. Consequently, he often has little patience for delays or traditional working methods.
In his interactions with neighbors and business partners, he maintains a professional and businesslike demeanor. He communicates clearly, expects reliability, and prefers direct arrangements without unnecessary detours. Ambiguous or inefficient working methods frustrate him quickly—a reaction that occasionally makes him appear distant or impatient.

text.name: gui_help_char_desc_20
text.text:
Role: Neighbour | Job: Farmer Age: 31–50 | Relationship: Known | Behaviour: Friendly, Supportive
Together with her husband, Emma runs a diversified agricultural operation specializing in the cultivation of vegetables and grains. A portion of their harvest is sold directly at local markets, while larger quantities are marketed through wholesalers and regional buyers. Their farm is well-organized and strongly focused on efficiency and quality.
In her interactions with neighbors and business partners, Emma is open, friendly, and helpful. She is regarded as someone who communicates in a straightforward manner and prefers finding solutions together rather than endlessly debating problems. She is particularly well-connected within the regional agricultural sector, where she maintains stable and fair relationships.
Her supportive nature is especially evident during stressful periods, such as planting or harvest seasons. Whenever possible, she lends a hand to other farms—or accepts help herself—without hesitation. She places great value on mutual support within the farming community and views collaboration as an essential part of her daily life.

text.name: gui_help_char_desc_19
text.text:
Role: Neighbour | Job: Farmer Age: 50+ | Relationship: Friend | Behaviour: Cautious, Neutral
Jacob is an experienced third-generation dairy farmer who runs a medium-sized family farm, which he manages jointly with his two sons. The farm has grown over many years and is firmly rooted in traditional structures, which he has evolved through careful adjustments to meet modern demands.
In his interactions with others, Jacob appears calm, composed, and rather reserved. He listens before speaking and rarely makes decisions on the spur of the moment. Particularly in business matters or dealings with neighbors, he takes care to avoid unnecessary risks and to steer clear of conflicts whenever possible.
His cautious nature is most evident when it comes to investments, new technologies, or major changes to farm operations. He favors proven methods and scrutinizes innovations thoroughly before integrating them into the business. This approach has enabled him to manage the farm with stability—even if, as a result, he is sometimes perceived as being too slow or conservative.

text.name: gui_help_char_desc_18
text.text:
Role: Neighbour | Job: Farmer Age: 31–50 | Relationship: Friend | Behaviour: Stressed, Uncooperative
Marcus is a local farmer who has managed a relatively small, highly traditional farm for many years. Most of his farm equipment is old; he repairs and continues to use it for as long as possible—a practice that increasingly complicates and slows down his daily work routine.
In his day-to-day interactions, Marcus often appears tense and somewhat unapproachable. The constant pressure stemming from equipment repairs, the time required for his work, and the ever-rising demands of modern farming have left him feeling weary and irritable over the years. He frequently regards new methods or modern technology with skepticism, having had negative experiences with past investments or operational changes.
He is not always cooperative in his dealings with neighbors and fellow farmers. Reaching agreements can be difficult, as he is quick to feel pressured or tends to resist changes to established procedures. His conflicts arise less from personal animosity and more from a sense of being overwhelmed—from the feeling that he is losing control of the situation.

text.name: gui_help_char_desc_17
text.text:
Role: Business Partner | Job: Agricultural Contractor Age: 31–50 | Relationship: Known | Behaviour: Uncooperative, Stressed
Paul runs an agricultural contracting business, offering services such as harvesting, manure spreading, tillage, and other fieldwork to local farms. His operation is heavily dependent on time constraints, weather conditions, and seasonal peak periods—factors that often make his daily routine hectic and highly demanding in terms of planning.
In his interactions with clients, Paul often comes across as curt and lacking in patience. He is focused squarely on completing jobs quickly and ensuring the efficient utilization of his machinery. He typically rejects lengthy discussions or last-minute changes to the workflow, as these place an additional strain on his already tight schedule.
Due to his heavy workload and constant pressure to meet deadlines, he frequently appears stressed and tense. Particularly during the harvest season or during periods of critical weather, he can seem uncooperative if operations do not proceed exactly according to plan. In such instances, his concern is less about personal conflicts and more about the absolute necessity of keeping his business running smoothly.
Despite his gruff demeanor, Paul is highly competent and experienced in all aspects of fieldwork. Provided that agreements are established clearly and well in advance, he works reliably and efficiently. However, he expects the same level of clarity and commitment from his business partners.

text.name: gui_help_char_desc_16
text.text:
Role: Business Partner | Job: Agricultural Machinery Dealer Age: 31–50 | Relationship: Close Friend | Behaviour: Professional, Friendly
Andy owns an agricultural machinery dealership and is well-connected throughout the entire regional agricultural sector. His business encompasses the sale, maintenance, and repair of farm machinery, as well as the procurement of spare parts. Through his daily work, he maintains close contact with farmers, workshops, and manufacturers, giving him a precise understanding of the practical demands of modern agriculture.
In his interactions with customers and business partners, he comes across as open, friendly, and solution-oriented. He knows how to communicate on equal footing and builds trust with operations of all sizes—from small family farms to large-scale agricultural estates. His primary focus is on finding practical, real-world solutions rather than engaging in lengthy discussions about theoretical possibilities.
As a business partner, Andy is regarded as both reliable and fair. He honors his commitments, demonstrates flexibility and goodwill whenever the situation permits, and strives to resolve issues quickly and without unnecessary bureaucracy. Particularly in cases of technical breakdowns or urgent repair needs, he is often available on short notice and dedicated to minimizing downtime for his customers.

text.name: gui_help_char_desc_15
text.text:
Role: Business Partner | Job: Grain Trader Age: 31–50 | Relationship: Known | Behaviour: Neutral, Suspicious
Susan works as an independent grain trader and travels regularly throughout the region to purchase grain from local farms and subsequently resell it to regional mills and processing plants. She knows the market well, monitors prices and yields closely, and reacts swiftly to shifts in supply and demand.
In her dealings with farmers and business partners, she comes across as matter-of-fact, reserved, and unemotional. She places great emphasis on clear agreements, verifiable quality, and transparent quantity specifications. She builds trust only slowly, as she relies on facts and reliable figures within her business environment—rather than on promises or personal impressions.
Susan is highly observant and quickly spots discrepancies in offers or deliveries. As a result, some perceive her as strict or suspicious, though this is simply part of her professional prudence. She favors clear contracts and places little stock in informal arrangements or spontaneous changes.

text.name: gui_help_char_desc_14
text.text:
Role: Official | Job: Mayor Age: 50+ | Relationship: Known | Behaviour: Professional, Authoritative
Martin has served as the community’s mayor for many years and is regarded as an experienced and influential figure within the town. He has been familiar with most of the region’s families, businesses, and developments for decades, and he places great importance on ensuring that the community projects an image of orderliness, stability, and sound organization to the outside world. He takes matters such as municipal finances, infrastructure, and the town’s public reputation particularly seriously.
In his interactions with others, Martin typically presents himself as composed, matter-of-fact, and decisive. He speaks calmly yet with authority, and he is accustomed to having his decisions command attention. He often conducts discussions diplomatically; however, he can be quite firm if rules are disregarded or if his decisions are openly challenged.

text.name: gui_help_char_desc_13
text.text:
Role: Neighbour | Job: Forester Age: 31–50 | Relationship: Friend | Behaviour: Cautious, Professional
Lukas works as a forester and is responsible for the care and management of the surrounding forest areas. His duties include, among other things, inspecting reforestation efforts, maintaining forest paths, monitoring nature conservation measures, and organizing and verifying hunting permits. He knows the region's forests intimately and spends a large portion of his daily life outdoors in nature.
Despite his rather reserved nature, he is regarded as a reliable and fair-minded individual. He is always happy to share his knowledge with friends, particularly when it comes to forestry work, timber, hunting, or nature conservation. He values honest and straightforward people and gets along well with those who act responsibly and keep their word.

text.name: gui_help_char_desc_11
text.text:
Role: Civilian Age: 31–50 | Relationship: Close Friend | Behaviour: Neutral, Friendly
Kyle works as a personal trainer and spends a large part of his daily life in the gym or engaged in athletic activities. Physical fitness, discipline, and a healthy lifestyle are important to him—without him ever coming across as preachy. He enjoys motivating others and usually manages to quickly win people over with his open and laid-back demeanor.
As a good friend of Joe, he is reliable and helpful—especially when someone needs practical support or motivation. He tends to focus on lifting people up and offering distractions rather than dwelling at length on difficulties. He generally avoids conflict, preferring instead to keep the atmosphere light.

text.name: gui_help_char_desc_10
text.text:
Role: Official Age: 50+ | Relationship: Strained | Behaviour: Authoritative, Cautious
Sharon works in the municipal administration, where she ensures clear procedures and harmonious community living. For farmers, this often involves matters such as nighttime quiet, cleanliness, and nuisances related to odors or noise. Outside of work, she generally tries to distance herself from constant organizing and oversight; she declines to engage in such discussions during her off-hours.
She is regarded as quiet, matter-of-fact, and rather reserved, though she can be friendly and humorous once she gets to know someone better. She typically approaches discussions in a sober and direct manner. In her free time, she enjoys reading, going for walks, or spending time in her garden. She tends to avoid large crowds and bustling environments. Order and reliability are important to her, both professionally and privately. Nevertheless, she makes a conscious effort to be more relaxed after work hours and to avoid constantly bringing her work home with her.

text.name: gui_help_char_desc_2
text.text:
Role: Family Member | Job: Farmer | Belongs to farm: Yes Age: 21–30 | Relationship: Family | Behaviour: Stressed
John Carter is the brother of Joe Carter—the player character—and grew up alongside him on the family farm. From an early age, farming was a natural and integral part of his life; consequently, he continues to strive to contribute to the daily farm routine as much as his abilities allow. However, due to a medical condition that has physically limited him for several years, he is now able to undertake only lighter and simpler tasks. This weighs on him more heavily than he openly reveals.
In the past, John was significantly more active in the fields and involved in physically demanding labor. Today, he struggles to come to terms with the fact that many things no longer function as they once did. He feels particularly useless—or like a burden to his family—when others have to take on the heavy work, even though no one intentionally makes him feel this way.

text.name: gui_help_char_desc_1
text.text:
Role: Family Member | Job: Farmer | Belongs to farm: Yes Age: 31–50 | Relationship: Family | Behaviour: Friendly, Professional, Supportive
Mary Carter is the wife of Joe Carter (the player) and is deeply integrated into both family life and the daily routine of the farm. Although she is not a formally trained farmer, she regularly pitches in with farm chores—whether in the barn or out in the fields—whenever a helping hand is needed. Particularly during busy periods, she steps in as a matter of course, striving to support her husband and family by taking care of things behind the scenes.
She is regarded as a quiet, warm-hearted, and dependable individual who typically approaches conflicts in a calm, objective, and patient manner. She is open and helpful toward others, though she also expects honesty and mutual respect in return. To strangers, she may initially appear somewhat reserved, but she quickly warms up once approached with friendliness.
In her daily life, she often handles organizational matters concerning the household, the family, and the farm. She makes a point of ensuring that, despite the heavy workload, a sense of family cohesion and a pleasant atmosphere are maintained. She generally attempts to navigate stressful situations with composure and pragmatic solutions, even if she occasionally takes on too much responsibility herself.

text.name: gui_help_conversations_body
text.text:
Neighbours are scattered around the map living their lives. Meeting them and talking is, alongside the realistic behaviour patterns, a central part of the mod.
How to start a conversation: 1. Walk up to a character on foot. When you are close enough (about 5 metres), you can perform the 'Start Conversation' action. 2. Press SHIFT+R to begin the conversation. 3. Listen to the conversation or read the subtitles. 4. When several replies appear, choose the line you want to say. Your choice shapes the conversation and how your relationship changes. 5. In the future you will be able to discuss both small talk and real actions (e.g. borrowing machinery).
Finding people: characters appear all over the map. A farmer in a vehicle is usually hard to reach, but if you approach on foot to within about 20 metres he will stop, switch off the machine and step out after a short while so you can talk to him.
Voiced conversations are played when a voice pack is installed; if not, you can simply read the text.

text.name: gui_help_controls_body
text.text: SHIFT+R: Start a conversation with a nearby person. If nobody is close, your phone opens instead. SHIFT+F3: Open the story and settings menu for Fields of Stories.

text.name: gui_help_relationships_body
text.text:
Every neighbour remembers how they feel about you. Good encounters build trust over time; bad ones cost you.
How it works: - Each character has a relationship level (starts at 1) and points that fill toward the next level. - A friendly conversation earns points (and a small matching cash reward). - When the points bar is full, the relationship level rises and the points reset for the next level. Higher levels need more points, but also grant higher bonuses when you reach the level. - Rude or dismissive replies can cost points. Sometimes even a seemingly friendly reply can trigger a negative feeling and worsen your relationship. Points never drop below zero and never lower a level you already reached.
Field contracts count too: a completed contract improves your relationship with that farmer, while failing, cancelling or letting a contract expire lowers it by the same amount.
At the end of a conversation, messages such as 'Relationship improved' or 'Relationship level increased' appear.

text.name: gui_help_story_body
text.text:
The Story tab gives you a quick overview of every character and your relationship with them. Open the menu with SHIFT+F3 and choose the Story tab.
On the left is a scrollable list of all neighbours - click a name to select it. On the right you see the selected character's portrait, your current relationship (such as Known, Friend or Family), the relationship level, and a progress bar showing how many relationship points you have earned toward the next level.
Use it to see at a glance who you already know well and who you should visit more often. See the Relationships topic for how points and levels are gained and lost.

text.name: gui_help_contracts_body
text.text:
Farmers sometimes have more fieldwork than they can manage and will call you for help. These offers are real, paid jobs on their fields. They may give you a whole series of fields to complete.
The call: 1. A farmer can call you during the day (roughly between 08:00 and 15:00). You hear a ring and see a call notice. 2. Open your phone and press Answer to hear the offer, or Decline / Esc to dismiss it. 3. The same farmer may try again later. There is a daily limit on contract calls (configurable in the game settings). If you do not answer a call at all, there will be a total of three further attempts to call you.
Choosing the work: in the phone conversation you can usually accept the entire field area or just the first half, and you can choose whether to use your own equipment or borrow the neighbour's machines. This requires that the farmer can spare his machines. Of course you can also simply decline the request.
Without borrowing: you complete the contract with your own machines. The work is paid per hectare and is done as soon as the field reaches the required state (progress is currently not calculated evenly).
These contracts are always requested by phone and will be visible in the usual mission menu only after accepting.
Reward and reputation: a completed contract pays you out and improves your relationship with the farmer. Failing, cancelling or expiry do the opposite.

text.name: gui_help_contracts_body2
text.text:
With borrowing: the neighbour lends you exactly the implements needed (weights and the tractor are not lent out, however). Where needed, borrowed machines start already filled with seed, spray, fertilizer, manure or slurry, which can be refilled at the neighbour's farm when required. Borrowing means slightly less pay than using your own equipment. Important: a contract with borrowed equipment stays at 99 percent until you drive every borrowed machine back to the neighbour's farm and detach it there. Only then is the contract considered complete.
If you borrow the equipment, you will find a marker on the map showing where the machines can be picked up. There you can refill the consumables (e.g. slurry, seed or herbicide) and, at the end of the contract, park the machines again.

text.name: gui_help_contract_cap_call_info
text.text: When a farmer needs a hand, you hear a ring and see an incoming-call notice (roughly between 08:00 and 15:00).

text.name: gui_help_contract_cap_call
text.text:
Open your phone and press Answer to hear the offer, or Decline / Esc to dismiss it.
You can adjust the number of job calls in the game settings. This refers to the number of farmers seeking help, not the number of calls.
Important: If you do not answer a call at all, there will be a total of three further attempts to call you.

text.name: gui_help_contract_cap_choices
text.text: In the call you pick the work: the whole field or just the first half, and whether to use your own equipment or borrow the neighbour's machines.

text.name: gui_help_contract_cap_borrow_map
text.text: If you borrow equipment, a map marker shows where to pick up the machines and where to return them afterwards.

text.name: gui_help_contract_cap_borrow_attach
text.text: At the farm you attach the borrowed implements and refill seed, spray, fertilizer or slurry there when needed.

text.name: gui_help_contract_cap_fieldwork
text.text: Do the fieldwork. With borrowed gear the contract stays at 99% until you drive every machine back to the farm and detach it.

text.name: gui_help_schedule_body
text.text:
The AI farmers are not just animated helpers. They genuinely plan and carry out fieldwork on their own land, day by day and season by season.
The daily plan: once per in-game day each farmer looks at the real state of their fields and builds a to-do list. They work through it during daylight, roughly from 06:00 until the evening, driving out, doing the work, and returning home afterwards.
What they do, in priority order: harvest ripe crops, then sow, then spray, then spread fertilizer, manure or slurry, then plough, harrow and cultivate. A task is only chosen when the field really needs it (the right crop, growth stage, ground state or weeds).
Following the seasons: sowing follows each crop's real planting calendar, and other jobs are tied to sensible months, so the visible work matches the time of year. When sowing, farmers rotate sensible crops (such as canola, wheat, barley, oats and soybean) and try to avoid the previous crop.
Outsourcing: each day a farmer can offer a particular task as a phone contract. These are requested by phone and, once accepted, are visible in the usual mission menu. If you do not accept it by the afternoon, he does it himself.
You can see a farmer's plan for the day in the Settings menu under Characters, including which tasks are reserved for you, offered as a contract, or handled by the farmer's own AI.

text.name: gui_help_privatelife_body
text.text:
Away from work, the characters have a life of their own. Every neighbour has a home they return to, and many follow personal routines throughout the day.
Things you might catch them doing: - Relaxing at home or tidying the house. - Spending time with friends, having a picnic, or playing with the kids. - Going for a walk, walking the dog, or reading a book. - Running everyday errands or refuelling the car. - Checking the vehicle at the roadside or driving to the workshop for repairs.
Routines depend on the time of day (morning, day, evening and night) as well as the character's role and job, so a farmer, the mayor and a civilian spend their free time differently.
This is exactly what makes the world feel alive: if you roam the map at different times you will meet people in all sorts of situations. Approaching them on foot lets you start a conversation and get to know them better.

text.name: gui_help_homebases_body
text.text:
A homebase is a character's home: the place where they live, return to in the evening, and can be found when they are off duty. Without a homebase a character does not really exist in the game – every neighbour needs at least one.
Two spawn points per character: each character should have at least two home places – one with the place size 'Character' (on foot; this is where the person stands and sleeps) and one with the place size 'Vehicle' (where their car parks). That way every neighbour has both an on-foot and a vehicle spawn point.
Creating home places: 1. Open the menu with SHIFT+F3 and go to the Settings tab. 2. Choose the matching place size at the top ('Character' or 'Vehicle'). 3. Stand on foot or park your vehicle exactly at the desired spot at the residence. 4. Open the menu and select 'Home of [Name]' in the place list to save the position. Alternatively, in the Characters section use 'Add nearest Homebase' to assign the nearest place directly to the selected character.
Removing existing home places: if you want to reset a character's residences, select them in the Characters section and use the 'Remove Homeplaces' button. This detaches all home places assigned to that character – the places themselves remain and can be reassigned afterwards.
Homebases are a good place to find someone, especially early in the morning or late at night.
Farmers: for them the homebase is also the farm and additionally needs space for machinery. You can learn how to design a farm properly in the 'Designing farms' chapter.

text.name: gui_help_homebasedesign_body
text.text:
This chapter is about the farms of the farmer characters. For a farmer, the homebase is also his farm: when the machines are not in use or out on a contract, they are parked there. So plan the farm generously, because a farmer owns many vehicles and implements (currently about 10 implements, 1 combine, 2 tractors and a car; the agricultural contractor even 23 implements, 6 self-propelled machines, 2 tractors and a car).
How to create the spots: open the menu with SHIFT+F3, go to the Settings tab, choose the matching place size at the top and save a 'Character homebase' at the desired spot (you will find the exact workflow in the 'Creating map places' chapter).
Which place sizes a farm needs: - 'Character' (on foot): for the farmer himself. - 'Vehicle': for cars and tractors. - 'Oversize vehicle': for combines and self-propelled harvesters. - 'Vehicle + implement': for implements and trailers. - 'Large area': for spacious situations at the farm, such as machine preparation. If you cannot find a spot for every machine, that is not a problem – depending on use they are parked or hidden.

text.name: gui_help_homebasedesign_body2
text.text:
Making the farm accessible: to borrow a farmer's machines, his farm has to be drivable. You need to be able to drive a vehicle up to the parked machines and back out again. So when placing farms, make sure no fences, gates, walls, trees or other objects block the entrance and exit.
Removing a gate: if a gate or door blocks the access, you can remove it directly in Fields of Stories. Stand or drive close to the gate (within about 20 m), open the menu with SHIFT+F3 on the Settings tab and click 'Remove nearest gate'. The nearest object whose node name contains 'gate' is permanently deleted from the map and stays gone after reloading. The following images show how removing the gate clears the access so the machines can be borrowed.

text.name: gui_help_voices_body
text.text:
Conversations can be fully voiced. The spoken audio comes as a separate voice pack so the mod itself stays small.
Installing a voice pack: download the voice pack (see the Mod´s GitHub page for the exact download location: AirFoxTwo/FS25_FieldsOfStories), then unpack it so that its 'conversations' folder sits in the mod's modSettings folder (modSettings/FS25_FIELDS_OF_STORIES). If the pack is missing or out of date, a warning dialog appears with the 'Check again' and 'Ignore' buttons. You can keep playing without it; conversations will then still show subtitles, just without spoken audio.
Languages: the mod is fully translated into English and German. The displayed text follows your game language automatically. Voice packs come in matching English and German versions, and the mod plays the audio matching the installed pack, otherwise matching the game language.
Conversation generation (advanced): each character has individual conversation texts with you. In total this adds up to over 10,000 written and voiced conversation parts. Every conversation was checked manually (at least for plausibility). Wording or grammar mistakes cannot, however, be ruled out.
Voice generation (advanced): each character has their own voice, generated from a text-to-speech voice design. These voices are then recorded for all conversation texts via voice cloning on a local NVIDIA RTX 5080. The plan is to improve quality through further optimization or even real voice actors, which however involves considerable effort.

text.name: gui_help_support_body
text.text:
Found a bug, or have an idea to make the mod better? Your feedback is very welcome.
Feedback, bug reports and feature requests are best sent through the mod's GitHub Page: AirFoxTwo/FS25_FieldsOfStories
When reporting a problem, it helps a lot to include: - a short description of what happened and what you expected, - the steps to reproduce it, - the map you are playing, - your log file if you can attach it.
Clear, detailed reports speed up fixes enormously. Thank you for helping improve Fields of Stories.

text.name: gui_mapinit_remove_mod_disclaimer
text.text: Note: After this, the game must be saved.

text.name: input_IAI3DReferencesDebug
text.text: Fields of Stories - Show debug points at all i3d reference nodes

text.name: input_IADumpUserAttributes
text.text: Fields of Stories - Dump user attributes of map nodes to log

text.name: gui_mapinit_add_nearest_homebase_info
text.text: The nearest place was assigned to character %s.

text.name: gui_mapinit_notify_remove_mod
text.text: Fields of Stories mod data was cleared. Save the game.

text.name: gui_mapinit_notify_nudge_vehicle_no_vehicle
text.text: Get in a vehicle to use this.

text.name: ia_field_outcome_mission_desc_short
text.text: Use a %s to finish this task.

text.name: ia_field_outcome_target_fertilizer
text.text: Target: mineral fertilizer stage %d on the field.

text.name: ia_field_outcome_target_slurry
text.text: Target: slurry (liquid manure) or fermentation residues (digestate) application to fertilization stage %d.

text.name: ia_field_outcome_target_manure
text.text: Target: solid manure spreading to fertilization stage %d.

text.name: ia_field_outcome_target_herbicide
text.text: Target: spray herbicide until weeds match the agreed state.

text.name: ia_field_outcome_target_seed
text.text: Target: %s established on the whole field.

text.name: ia_field_outcome_mission_finished
text.text: Fields of Stories field contract completed.

text.name: ia_field_outcome_mission_failed
text.text: Fields of Stories field contract ended without success.

text.name: ia_mission_borrow_refill_at_farm
text.text: Borrowed equipment is empty — refill it at %s's farm.

text.name: ia_mission_borrow_return_to_farm
text.text: Field work is done — return the borrowed equipment to %s's farm (detach it at the yard).

text.name: ia_settings_contractCallsPerDay_info
text.text: Maximum number of contract phone calls per in-game day across all neighbours.
